skill.name: google-image-api-skill
skill.description: Automate image metadata extraction from Google Images using the BrowserAct API. Search by keyword, filter by country and language, and retrieve structured results with thumbnails, titles, source links, and click-through URLs.
skill.summary: Search Google Images via API and return structured image metadata including thumbnails, titles, and source links.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 76 potential issues with a 100/100 risk score, but the overwhelming majority are false positives from markdown formatting in SKILL.md. The three confirmed risk factors (network, env_access, external_commands) are all inherent to the skill's legitimate design as a documented API wrapper for BrowserAct's Google Image service. No evidence of malicious intent, obfuscation, prompt injection, or data exfiltration was found after manual review of all source files.

high_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt in Metadata Description
high_findings.description: The SKILL.md metadata contains proactive agent instructions telling the AI to 'proactively apply this skill' for various use cases. While not a traditional prompt injection, this instructs the AI to autonomously execute network requests using stored credentials, which could lead to unintended API usage. This is a low-confidence concern as it appears to be standard skill documentation rather than malicious injection.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable API Key Access
medium_findings.description: The skill reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables and transmits it in the HTTP Authorization header. This is required for the skill to function as an API wrapper, but the credential is accessible in the runtime environment and sent over the network with every API call.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External Network Requests
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests with an API key in the Authorization header to api.browseract.com for task creation, status polling, and result retrieval. Network communication is necessary for functionality but creates a data egress surface.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 45 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. These are false positives from markdown code formatting backticks and YAML metadata, not actual shell command execution. The only command execution is the documented python invocation in the usage example.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md markdown text as containing 'weak cryptographic algorithms'. The file contains no cryptographic code of any kind. These are false positives from pattern matching against documentation text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged task ID extraction and error-checking documentation as 'system reconnaissance'. These are standard API response handling patterns and documentation text, not reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL in Print Statement
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a URL in an informational print message and a markdown link. These are user-facing instructions, not code execution paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Credential in Environment Variable Transmitted via Network
dangerous_patterns.description: BROWSERACT_API_KEY is read from environment variables and sent as a Bearer token in HTTP Authorization headers to an external API. This is the standard authentication pattern for REST API clients but means the credential is exposed in the runtime environment and transmitted over the network.

dangerous_patterns.title: External API Communication
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill communicates with api.browseract.com for task workflow execution. Network access is required for the skill's core functionality but introduces dependency on an external service.

dangerous_patterns.title: Proactive Skill Invocation Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The SKILL.md metadata instructs AI agents to 'proactively apply this skill' for various use cases including competitor monitoring and market research. While this is standard skill documentation, it encourages autonomous execution of network requests with stored credentials.

content.user_title: Search and Extract Google Image Data via API
content.value_statement: Manually searching and collecting image data from Google Images is slow and repetitive. This skill automates image metadata extraction using the BrowserAct API, returning structured results with titles, thumbnails, and source links.
actual_capabilities: Search Google Images by keyword with configurable country and language settings
Extract structured image metadata including thumbnails, titles, source names, and click-through URLs
Configure scroll depth and maximum result count to control data volume
Poll task execution status with timestamped progress logging until completion
Detect authorization errors and provide clear user guidance for API key issues
Support nine countries and eight languages for localized image searches
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key configured in the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Dependent on BrowserAct API availability and may take several minutes for large result sets
Extracts metadata only, does not download or save actual image files
Limited to the fields and structure provided by the BrowserAct Google Image API template

use_cases.title: Visual Content Research
use_cases.description: Collect image metadata for creative projects or design inspiration by searching with specific keywords across different languages and regions.

use_cases.title: Competitor Visual Asset Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track competitor product images and brand appearances in Google Image results across multiple countries for competitive analysis.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Product Discovery
use_cases.description: Find product images and click-through URLs to identify where specific products are sold in different regions and markets.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Keyword Image Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google Images for [keyword] and show me the image results with thumbnails, titles, and source links.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wanting to search images with a single keyword

prompt_templates.title: Localized Image Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google Images for [keyword] in country [code] with language [code]. Show the top results with metadata.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs region-specific image results for a particular market

prompt_templates.title: Custom Volume Image Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Google Image search for [keyword] in country [code]. Set scroll count to [number] and max items to [number]. Return the full structured results.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to build a visual dataset with specific depth and volume parameters

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Market Visual Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google Images for [brand or product] across countries us, gb, jp, and de. Compare the image results from each market and describe the differences.
prompt_templates.scenario: User performing competitive visual analysis across multiple international markets

output_examples.input: Search for vintage cameras in Japan
output_examples.output: The search returned 50 image results from Google Images (Japan, Japanese language). Each result includes a thumbnail URL, title text, source site name, source logo URL, click-through link, and whether the result is a product listing.

output_examples.input: Compare coffee product images across us and fr markets
output_examples.output: US results show 30 product-style coffee images from major retailers with clear packaging shots. France results show 28 images with more variety in presentation style, including cafe settings and artisan branding. All results include source names, logos, and click-through links.

output_examples.input: Find images for sustainable fashion trends
output_examples.output: Extracted 45 image results from Google Images. Results include thumbnail previews, source attribution from fashion publications and brand sites, and click-through URLs. The most common sources are Vogue, Elle, and brand lookbooks.

best_practices: Set a meaningful scroll count to balance result coverage and execution time, starting with 3-5 scrolls for most searches
Use specific country and language codes to localize image results for accurate regional market analysis
Verify the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before running the skill to avoid authorization errors
anti_patterns: Do not set scroll count higher than necessary as it increases execution time without proportional benefit beyond saturation
Do not use this skill to download or store image files, as it only extracts metadata URLs and text descriptions
Do not retry more than once on authorization errors because the API key needs manual renewal by the user

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key set as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Does this skill download images?
faq.answer: No. It extracts metadata such as thumbnail URLs, titles, source names, and click-through links from Google Images.

faq.question: Which countries and languages are supported?
faq.answer: Countries: us, gb, ca, au, de, fr, es, jp, kr. Languages: en, zh-CN, zh-TW, ja, ko, fr, de, es.

faq.question: How long does a search take?
faq.answer: The task can take several minutes depending on scroll depth and result count. The script logs progress with timestamps.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The script detects invalid authorization and stops. Provide a valid BrowserAct API key before retrying.

faq.question: Can I use this for market research?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports multi-country searches and returns structured metadata for competitive analysis and trend tracking.
